name: Superset
description: An open-source terminal for parallel coding agents that lets developers run up to 10 AI agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) simultaneously across different git worktrees. Manages isolated working environments, handles environment variables, and provides a GUI to orchestrate multiple CLI-based coding agents.
community_quotes: A turbocharged terminal that can 10x development workflow
Users are choosing Superset over traditional IDEs or replacing their terminals entirely
Great for offloading minor refactoring work and non-overlapping features
The real bottleneck isn't human review, it's unstructured review - parallel agents need tight contracts
Manages the mess of having multiple windows and terminal tabs open for each task

name: JetBrains Air
description: An agentic development environment for delegating coding tasks to multiple AI agents and running them concurrently. Built on 26 years of JetBrains IDE experience, Air lets developers switch agents freely, run tasks concurrently, and provides integrated tools like terminal, Git client, and built-in preview.
community_quotes: Air Launches as Public Preview - A new wave of dev tooling built on 26 years of experience
Air helps you navigate your codebase with precise context instead of pasted text blobs
Supports multiple agent providers: Codex, Claude Agent, Gemini CLI, and Junie out of the box
Focuses on agent orchestration without replacing existing development workflows
The part that's most different is storage - filesystems as first-class primitives separate from task lifecycle

name: Terminal Use
description: A deployment platform for filesystem-based AI agents (coding agents, research agents, document processing) that provides sandboxed environments with first-class filesystem primitives. Like 'Vercel for agents' - packages agent code from repos, serves via clean API/SDK, handles state persistence, and supports Claude Agent SDK and Codex SDK.
community_quotes: The decoupled filesystem model is the right primitive for agent workloads
Achieved the highest upvoted Hacker News launch in YC W26 with 131 upvotes and 83 comments
The version pinning approach for existing tasks is really useful in practice
A whole category of yak-shaving removed for agent deployment infrastructure
The separation of gather context → synthesize → act is critical in production agent systems

name: Junie CLI
description: An LLM-agnostic coding agent from JetBrains that works in any environment - terminal, any IDE, CI/CD pipelines, and GitHub/GitLab. Features real-time prompting and codebase intelligence, bringing JetBrains-quality AI assistance to development workflows.
community_quotes: Junie CLI, the LLM-agnostic coding agent, is now in Beta
Brings JetBrains quality to any environment
Can be used directly from terminal, inside any IDE, in CI/CD pipelines, and on GitHub or GitLab
Representing a fundamental paradigm shift in JetBrains' approach to AI-assisted development
LLM-agnostic design gives developers flexibility in model choice

name: Anthropic Code Review
description: An AI-powered code reviewer from Anthropic designed to catch bugs in AI-generated code before they enter codebases. A multi-agent system that automatically flags logic errors, targeting enterprise developers dealing with the influx of AI-generated code.
community_quotes: Anthropic's solution is an AI reviewer designed to catch bugs before they make it into software codebases
Specifically targets the problem of checking the flood of AI-generated code
Automatically identifies potential issues before vulnerabilities enter the codebase
Aims to alleviate efficiency bottlenecks in modern software development
Represents Anthropic's strategic move to capture more of the developer workflow
